notes: Today is Sunday 2026-05-17. US markets are closed, so the price snapshot is Friday May 15's confirmed close. Sources cross-checked: Yahoo Finance and Morningstar agree at $422.24 (-4.75%); GuruFocus reports $424.45 (-4.8%); Cryptonomist/Foreign Policy Journal cite $428.72 (-3.73%). The $6.48 spread exceeds the $2 cross-check threshold, so per policy the lowest figure ($422.24) is used and the discrepancy is flagged here.

news.sentiment_summary: Sentiment heading into Monday's open has soured. The weekend has been spent re-reading Friday's headline triple-whammy — no China FSD greenlight from the Trump-Xi summit, the CFO's confirmation of negative free cash flow for the rest of 2026, and a 218,868-vehicle NHTSA rearview-camera recall — and the digestion is not flattering to bulls. The robotaxi growth story is still real (the fleet has more than quadrupled in six weeks and Optimus V3 production lines are being installed at Fremont), but with a trailing P/E around 416x there is almost no margin for the kind of execution slip that Q1's 50,000-unit inventory build represents. Wall Street's consensus target of $403.86 now sits 4% below Friday's close — the first time in six weeks that analysts have collectively become more cautious than the market.

items.headline: Wall Street Consensus Target Falls Below Spot Price for First Time in 6 Weeks
items.summary: After Friday's 4.75% selloff, Tesla's last close ($422.24) is now 4% above the average analyst 12-month price target of $403.86 (per TipRanks aggregation of 29 analysts). That is the first time since the early-April rally began that the Street's consensus has been less bullish than the market itself. The implication: Wall Street as a whole now thinks the stock has more downside than upside over the next year, even if individual bulls like Wedbush ($600) and bears like GLJ ($25) continue to disagree wildly.

items.headline: Optimus V3 Production Lines Being Installed at Fremont; Reveal Slated for Late July
items.summary: Tesla has begun installing first-generation Optimus humanoid robot production lines at its Fremont, California facility, with the V3 robot expected to be unveiled in late July or early August 2026 alongside Q2 earnings. Initial capacity is targeted at 1 million units a year, scaling to 10 million from a second factory at Giga Texas by 2027. V3 confirmed specs: 37 joints (9 more than V2), 1.2 m/s walking speed, and 22-degree-of-freedom dexterous hands with sub-millimeter precision. This is the strongest tangible Optimus update Tesla has provided since the platform was announced.

items.headline: Robotaxi Crash Tally Disclosed: 17 Incidents Since Austin Launch
items.summary: Newly unredacted NHTSA filings show Tesla's unsupervised robotaxi fleet has recorded 17 crashes since the Austin launch in mid-2025. No fatalities or serious injuries are reported, but reviewers flagged repeated navigation glitches and long wait times during recent test rides in Dallas and Houston. The disclosure lands a week before Tesla is expected to confirm new city launches in Phoenix, Miami, Orlando, Tampa, and Las Vegas — and it gives regulators ammunition just as Tesla pushes for FSD approvals in Europe and (still) China.

items.headline: Cybercab Car Wash Construction Begins Near Las Vegas
items.summary: Tesla has broken ground on a dedicated Cybercab car wash facility in Clark County, Nevada, just outside Las Vegas — a small but symbolically important sign that the company is building physical infrastructure for the robotaxi fleet's seven-city rollout. The Las Vegas launch is one of five new cities Tesla has lined up for the second half of 2026, alongside Phoenix, Miami, Orlando, and Tampa. Bulls see this as proof that the deployment is real, not a slideshow.

items.headline: 30-Year Treasury Yield Holds Above 5% — Pressure on All Long-Duration Growth Names
items.summary: The 30-year US Treasury yield closed Friday above 5% for the second consecutive session, a level that materially raises the discount rate applied to long-duration growth stocks. For Tesla — whose valuation rests heavily on cash flows from robotaxi, Optimus, and FSD that mostly arrive after 2028 — higher long rates are a direct headwind. The bond move helped to amplify Friday's TSLA decline beyond what the China FSD news alone would have justified.

analysis_7d.macd_signal: Bullish but rolling over — the MACD line has bent sharply back toward the signal line and a bearish crossover is plausible early next week
analysis_7d.ma_cross_status: Golden Cross intact — the 50-day MA ($416.75) sits well above the 200-day MA ($383.23), the long-term bullish setup is preserved

Down day on below-average volume suggests profit-taking rather than panic selling
analysis_7d.summary: Tesla's week was a textbook 'rally, blow-off, reversal' pattern. The stock climbed steadily from around $398 to a Wednesday high of $445 on China-summit optimism, then surrendered most of those gains in a single Friday session after the summit ended without the expected FSD approval. The encouraging part is that the long-term setup — Golden Cross intact, price comfortably above the 200-day average — is undisturbed, and Friday's drop happened on lighter-than-usual volume which argues against a panic exit. The discouraging part is that the short-term picture has clearly turned: price is below its 5-day average, RSI has cooled to a flat 44, and MACD momentum is rolling over. Next week's key battle is the $416-$418 zone where the 50-day MA and recent support converge; defending that holds the medium-term trend, breaking it opens a path back to the high-$390s.

analysis_30d.trend: broad uptrend cooling at the highs

key_levels.support_reason: Where the 50-day moving average ($416.75) and the early-May breakout zone converge — the natural trampoline if selling continues. A weekly close below this level would be the first real crack in the rebound story.
key_levels.resistance_reason: Last week's intraday rally stalled here twice. Sellers reappeared at $445-$450 on Wednesday and Thursday before the Friday reversal — a glass ceiling that must be cleared before bulls can target the April highs near $466.

analysis_30d.rsi_label: Neutral — Friday's pullback cooled an otherwise stretched month
analysis_30d.macd_signal: Bullish but weakening — the daily MACD histogram is shrinking and a crossover would not be a surprise
notable_events: Apr 22: Q1 2026 earnings — EPS beat ($0.41 vs $0.36 est) and revenue beat ($22.39B vs $22.35B est), but deliveries missed at 358K and inventory swelled by more than 50K units (largest single-quarter build in Tesla history)
May 02-09: Robotaxi rally — stock climbed from $392 to $434 as fleet expanded from 9 to 39 vehicles and Dallas/Houston went live
May 12: Mid-week pullback to $412 on Dallas/Houston operational glitches and Panasonic battery delays
May 13-14: Sharp rebound to $443 on RBC's reiteration of Outperform (FSD subs +51% YoY)
May 15: -4.75% single-day reversal after Trump-Xi Beijing summit ended without China FSD approval; 30-year Treasury yield breaking 5% added discount-rate pressure
analysis_30d.summary: The 30-day picture is still a recovery story — Tesla is up 16.5% — but it is increasingly a story with cracks visible at the edges. The narrative drivers (Q1 earnings beat, robotaxi fleet quadrupling, FSD subs +51%) remain genuinely powerful, but each successive rally leg has met more resistance and Friday's reversal was the first clear sign that the easy money has been made. The stock is now caught in a $416-$450 consolidation range bordered below by the 50-day moving average and above by last week's failed breakout zone. The trend remains 'up' but it has lost its swagger; until either China FSD approval lands or Q2 numbers force a re-rate, expect choppier two-way action and smaller daily moves.

outlook.recent_rating_change: RBC Capital reiterated Outperform on May 12 citing 51% YoY growth in FSD subscriptions; Wedbush reiterated $600 high target on April 23; Morgan Stanley sits at $425 (raised from $410 in December)

catalysts.detail: Street consensus sits at 390K-420K deliveries, a 9-17% sequential lift from Q1's 358K. Anything below 390K would confirm the slowdown thesis and likely re-test recent lows; a number above 420K would re-energise the bull case.
EPS estimate $0.42 on $24.52B revenue. Wall Street wants auto gross margin back above 17% and visible early Cybercab revenue. A miss on either undermines the $25B capex case.

First-gen Optimus production lines are being installed at Fremont now, with the V3 unveiling expected alongside Q2 earnings. A credible demo and a real production timeline would re-anchor the long-term AI thesis.
Approval would let Tesla monetise FSD in the world's largest EV market and would reverse Friday's selloff overnight. Rejection — or further silence — is now largely priced in.

catalysts.timing: Phoenix, Miami, Orlando, Tampa, Las Vegas — rolling H2 2026
catalysts.detail: Each new city is a tangible proof point. But the freshly disclosed 17-crash tally raises the cost of any safety incident in a high-profile launch market.

macro_factors.detail: 30-year Treasury yields above 5% raise the discount rate applied to Tesla's late-decade cash flows from robotaxi and Optimus — a direct valuation headwind for AI-premium names
The Beijing summit produced no FSD deal and signals a colder China backdrop, threatening Tesla's Shanghai-built export volumes and its already-shrunken 3% China NEV market share

Europe deliveries fell ~49% YoY in Q1 on subsidy cuts and Chinese EV competition; Tesla's 50K+ inventory build raises the odds of another round of price cuts to clear stock, which would compress margins

outlook.summary: The next three to six months are about whether Tesla can turn narrative into numbers — and for the first time in six weeks, Wall Street as a whole is sceptical. The consensus 12-month target of $403.86 now sits 4% below Friday's close, meaning the median analyst expects negative returns from here. Bulls will point to genuine progress: the robotaxi fleet is real and expanding, Optimus V3 production lines are being installed, FSD subscriptions are growing 51% YoY. Bears will counter with a 416x trailing P/E, 50K+ inventory build, no China FSD, and negative free cash flow for the rest of 2026. The Q2 delivery report in early July is the next hard catalyst; until then expect choppy trading inside a $400-$450 range, with breakouts requiring either a China FSD surprise (bull case) or a robotaxi safety incident (bear case).

recommendation.reasoning: The signal balance has tilted incrementally more bearish over the weekend but not enough to flip the call. Bullish: the Golden Cross is intact, price is comfortably above both the 50-day and 200-day moving averages, RSI is neutral with room to move either way, and the fundamental robotaxi/Optimus/FSD story keeps adding tangible proof points (39-vehicle fleet, V3 production lines installed, Cybercab car wash breaking ground). Bearish: Friday's -4.75% drop pushed price below the 5-day MA and broke the rally structure, MACD momentum is rolling over, the analyst consensus target ($403.86) now sits below the spot price for the first time in six weeks, 30-year yields above 5% are a direct headwind to long-duration growth, and Tesla still has no China FSD approval despite a high-profile summit attempt. Confidence is Medium because both scenarios remain credible and a single news catalyst (China FSD landing, or a robotaxi safety incident) could resolve the picture either way within days. What flips this call: a decisive break above $450 with above-average volume turns this BUY; a weekly close below $416 (the 50-day MA) turns it SELL.
recommendation.key_risk: A failure to defend the $416 50-day moving average next week would close the medium-term cushion that has supported the rebound since early May, opening a probable retest of the high-$390s where the early-May breakout originated. Given that Wall Street's consensus target is already below the spot price, there is little analyst-target support to cushion any deeper drawdown.
